uploadLockfileCard.title: Upload Lockfile
uploadLockfileCard.description: Upload your package-lock.json file to analyze dependency impact.
uploadLockfileCard.analyzedSuffix: analyzed. Click or drop to replace it.
uploadLockfileCard.error: Analysis failed. Please upload a valid package-lock.json file.

dependencyGraphCard.title: Dependency Graph
dependencyGraphCard.description: The graph shows how packages connect and which ones have the highest structural impact on your project.
dependencyGraphCard.mockModeNote: This live demo uses mock data. The production backend is intentionally disabled to avoid hosting costs.
dependencyGraphCard.mockModeNoteSuffix: Full functionality is available in the
dependencyGraphCard.emptyState: Graph visualization will render here after analysis

riskTableCard.title: Top Dependency Impact Packages
riskTableCard.description: Packages with the highest structural dependency impact in your graph.
riskTableCard.rankingNote: Packages are ranked by dependency impact, not by vulnerability severity.

headers.riskTooltip: Derived only from dependency graph position and reach. It does not indicate known vulnerabilities.
headers.reasonTooltip: This score estimates how many packages are affected if this package changes. It reflects structural impact only.
headers.vulnerabilitiesTooltip: Number of known security vulnerabilities for this exact package version.
headers.highlightTooltip: Highlight this package and dependents in the graph; reveals structural impact and security context.

riskTableCard.emptyTable: Upload a package-lock.json to populate this table
riskTableCard.scrollToTopAriaLabel: Scroll to top of risk table

aiExplanationPanel.title: Dependency Impact & Security Context
aiExplanationPanel.structuralSectionHelper: Why this package matters structurally in the dependency graph.
aiExplanationPanel.securitySectionHelper: Security issues are shown separately from structural impact.

mockApi.placeholderExplanation: [Mock Placeholder] This package contributes to overall dependency risk due to its breadth and depth in the project's dependency tree. In production, real AI-generated insights will be provided. Run the backend to see them.
